title: DRMN vs Calm: Sleep Sounds vs Meditation Apps (2026)
description: Calm focuses on guided meditation and subscriptions. DRMN is a portable sound machine for iPhone. Compare features, pricing, and sleep vs mindfulness.
excerpt: Calm and DRMN both help you wind down, but they solve different problems. Here is a factual comparison of sleep sounds vs meditation apps on iPhone.
author: Vladimir Ivakhnenko

hero.alt: iPhone on a nightstand playing ambient sleep sounds beside a dim lamp

faq.question: Is DRMN a good alternative to Calm for sleep?
faq.answer: DRMN is better suited if you mainly want steady ambient sounds—white noise, rain, fan hum—with offline playback and sound mixing. Calm is better if you want guided meditations, sleep stories, and a broader wellness library behind a subscription.

faq.question: Does Calm have white noise?
faq.answer: Calm includes some ambient and nature sounds, but its core product is guided content and sleep stories. DRMN is built around a sound-machine model with mixing, a sleep timer, and offline tracks designed for masking bedroom noise.

faq.question: Which app is cheaper for sleep sounds only?
faq.answer: DRMN is free to download with a one-hour timer, offline mode, and sound mixing in the free tier. Calm typically requires a paid subscription (around $69.99/year in the U.S.) to unlock most sleep and meditation content after a trial.

faq.question: Can I use both Calm and DRMN?
faq.answer: Yes. Some people use Calm for guided wind-down routines and DRMN for all-night masking or offline travel. They are complementary rather than direct substitutes for every use case.

faq.question: Which app works better offline?
faq.answer: DRMN is designed for downloaded tracks and battery-optimized all-night playback without internet. Calm supports offline downloads for premium subscribers, but the experience is centered on streaming guided sessions.

content:
DRMN is an iOS sleep sound machine app built around steady ambient audio—white noise, rain, fan sounds, and nature tracks with mixing and offline playback. Calm is a meditation and wellness app known for guided sessions, sleep stories, and breathing exercises behind a subscription.

Both can help you relax at night, but they are not the same category of product. This comparison focuses on sleep and background sound use cases, based on publicly listed App Store features and pricing as of 2026.

Quick summary

  • Choose DRMN if you want a portable sound machine: masking traffic, mixing rain + fan, offline travel, baby sleep sounds, and a generous free tier.
  • Choose Calm if you want guided meditation, celebrity sleep stories, mood programs, and a full mindfulness platform.

DRMN vs Calm: feature comparison

FeatureDRMNCalm
Primary focusAmbient sleep & focus soundsGuided meditation & wellness
White / brown noiseCore library + mixingSome ambient tracks; not the main product
Guided meditationNo guided coursesExtensive guided library
Sleep storiesNo narrated storiesLarge catalog (often premium)
Sound mixingYes, in free tierLimited compared to dedicated sound apps
Offline playbackYes; battery-optimizedYes for premium downloads
Sleep timerAuto-fade timer (1 hr free)Varies by content type
Baby sleep soundsHair dryer, vacuum, keyboard-style tracksGeneral calm audio; not nursery-specific
Free tierCore sounds, 1-hour timer, mixing, offlineLimited free content; most sleep audio is premium
Typical pricingFree + optional lifetime ($19.99) or trial sub~$69.99/year subscription (U.S., 2026)
App Store rating4.9/5 (10 reviews)4.8/5 (1M+ ratings)

When DRMN is the better fit

DRMN behaves like a bedside sound machine on your iPhone. That matters if:

  • Your main problem is unpredictable noise—neighbors, traffic, snoring, or a creaky house.
  • You want steady, non-verbal audio that stays in the background instead of a voice telling a story.
  • You need offline playback on a plane, camping trip, or bedroom with weak Wi‑Fi.
  • You like to mix sounds—for example, brown noise under gentle rain.
  • You prefer trying core features without a subscription.

In App Store reviews, DRMN users often mention faster sleep onset when background noise is the main issue. Individual results vary; sound masking works best alongside consistent bedtime habits.

When Calm is the better fit

Calm shines when you want structured mindfulness rather than only background noise:

  • Guided body scans, anxiety programs, and daily meditation streaks.
  • Sleep stories narrated by recognizable voices for people who dislike static noise.
  • Breathing exercises and mood content beyond bedtime.
  • A single subscription covering meditation, movement, and music.

If you already pay for Calm and use sleep stories nightly, you may not need a second app—unless pure masking or mixing still leaves gaps.

Pricing reality check

Calm’s value proposition is breadth: meditation, music, classes, and stories in one subscription. DRMN’s value proposition is depth in ambient sound tooling with a free tier that includes practical sleep features (timer, offline, mixing).

For sleep-sound-only use cases, paying ~$70/year for Calm may be more than you need. DRMN’s optional lifetime purchase targets users who want sound-machine functionality without ongoing meditation content.

Science note: masking vs guided relaxation

Research on white noise and sound masking suggests steady background audio can reduce sleep disruption from sudden environmental sounds—especially in noisy settings. Guided meditation studies show benefits for stress reduction and sleep quality through relaxation, not acoustic masking alone.

Neither approach replaces medical care for chronic insomnia or sleep apnea. Pick the tool that matches your mechanism: mask noise (DRMN) vs guided wind-down (Calm).

Practical recommendation

  1. Try DRMN free if noise masking is your primary goal. Run a one-week trial at modest volume (40–50 dB).
  2. Keep Calm if you rely on guided sessions or sleep stories and still find them effective.
  3. Combine both only if you have distinct needs—e.g., a 10-minute Calm wind-down, then DRMN for masking through the night.

See also: BetterSleep Alternative for iPhone and Best White Noise Apps for iPhone Compared.

Last updated: July 2, 2026. Feature and pricing details reflect publicly listed App Store information and may change.
